body_txt: Named after an old joke , which seems at first blush to be a pair of unrelated jokes. At the end of the first joke, a brick is tossed away, leaving the confused listener without a punchline . At the end of the second joke, the brick returns and the listener falls on the floor laughing . For bonus points, the teller can tell an actual unrelated joke in between. Sometimes, the Brick Joke structure of introducing a seemingly irrelevant feature, only to return to it much later , after the audience has largely forgotten about it, can be used for drama as well as comedy; when that feature was a joke, it's known as Chekhov's Gag . Popularized in early 20th-century Comic Strips by Krazy Kat . In a masterclass on playwriting, Alan Ayckbourn mentions this trope specifically, calling it "The Plant". Early on, one of his two demonstration actors mentions that he has an urge to sneeze when sexually aroused. Then at the very end: The Stinger usually consists of one. Contrast All There in the Stinger , when the Stinger contains information that's vital to understanding the plot, and What Happened to the Mouse? , when a subject just just suddenly disappears in the story without any potential comback. Compare and Contrast with the Overly Long Gag , where the humor is in how long it takes to get to the punchline, as well as Big-Lipped Alligator Moment , which the setup of a Brick Joke can easily resemble until the punchline is delivered. note At which point, it's disqualified from being a BLAM, since it got referenced after the initial moment was over. See also Late to the Punchline , which is where a character who doesn't get a joke finally gets it, making it a kind of Brick Joke for that character. See Comeback Tomorrow if the character finally delivers their late comeback to a confused opponent. See also Something We Forgot and "Shaggy Dog" Story . May overlap with Insult Accuracy Acceptance if an insult given earlier in a work is acknowledged as true later on. May overlap with Punishment Pending , when someone is warned they will be punished for a screw up, but nothing seemingly happens until much later.
